note: SNG-191. TWO CLOCKS, DIFFERENT UNITS. Character time is days and seasons — personal, human, advanced by timeOps, uncapped. World time is a monotonic COUNT — shared, real-time-derived, never rewound. They do not share a unit ON PURPOSE: two clocks in the same unit invite arithmetic, two clocks in different units simply coexist.
ruling: Erik 2026-07-19: a journey advances ONLY the traveller's clock. The world moves on its own and the tick reports what was missed.

unit.granularity: ~1 count per hour of real time
unit.why: A week away reads as ~170 — a number that has plainly BUILT. Fine enough to accumulate, coarse enough to mean something.

custody.keepsNote: The Hourkeepers KEEP the time — custody, authority, continuity. It is their craft and their charge.
custody.measuresNote: The Lattice-Cities maintain the MEASUREMENT, because measurement is HOW a thing is kept: the standard, the instruments, the calibration that makes one count mean the same thing in two places.
custody.whyBoth: Neither owns it alone, and that division is why it is trusted — the people who hold the count are not the people who verify it. Erik's ruling 2026-07-19.

hourkeeper.note: The canonical form. Theirs to keep, and they state it without flourish.

lattice.note: The standard, given precisely and a little pedantically. They will correct your rounding.

ashwarden.note: A bell, rung. Counting is how you attend an ending, and Cairnhold already keeps a bench beside the gate.

enginewright.note: The Engine turns. They count its turning and consider every other method approximate.

marcher.note: You count what you must stand.

rootkin.note: They mark growth, not machinery — and are unbothered that this makes them imprecise.

umbral.note: They count what everyone else stops counting at.

churnfolk.note: They refuse to keep a steady one and are entirely unbothered. Asked twice, you get two answers, both delivered with confidence.

mason.note: A course of stone laid. What is counted should be something you could point at.

stillhold.note: They count intervals of quiet held, which is the only thing they consider worth counting.

verist.note: The plain word. They will not dress it up and think less of those who do.

usage.gmNarratesFrom: CHARACTER time, always — days, seasons, time of day.
usage.worldCountAppears: On news, on shared-canon stamps, and in a people's own idiom when a character asks or an NPC would say it. NEVER as a bare day-number in prose.
usage.why: There is no longer a world day-number available to invent with. This is the root fix for the invented 'World-day 23' entries that reached durable records (SNG-189 §2, SNG-190 §5).

partySync: SNG-191 §3, Erik's ruling: forming a party syncs the members' CHARACTER clocks — not world time. From the join forward they advance together. Their PASTS stay different lengths, and that is biography, not an error. Nothing is retconned.
